high.title: Potential SQL Injection
high.description: A SQL query appears to be constructed using string concatenation or interpolation with user-controlled data. This can lead to SQL injection if the input is not properly sanitized.
high.code_snippet:
with open(self.db_path, "w") as f:
                json.dump({"vulnerabilities": vulnerabilities}, f, indent=2)
            logger.info(f"Updated vulnerability database with {len(vulnerabilities)} entries.")
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to update vulnerability database: {e}")
high.remediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead of string concatenation. Escape user inputs if dynamic SQL is unavoidable.

high.title: Potential SQL Injection
high.description: A SQL query appears to be constructed using string concatenation or interpolation with user-controlled data. This can lead to SQL injection if the input is not properly sanitized.
high.remediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead of string concatenation. Escape user inputs if dynamic SQL is unavoidable.

high.remediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead of string concatenation. Escape user inputs if dynamic SQL is unavoidable.

high.title: Potential Reflected XSS
high.description: User input appears to be directly output to the HTML response without proper encoding or sanitization. This can lead to reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) if the input contains malicious scripts.
high.code_snippet:
"""
    Detects reflected XSS patterns:
    - Output of user input without proper encoding (e.g., echo $_GET['x'])
    - Direct rendering of request parameters in HTML
    """
high.remediation: Always escape output based on the context (HTML, JavaScript, URL, etc.). Use a templating engine that auto-escapes, or manually apply functions like htmlspecialchars() in PHP, or equivalent in other languages.

high.title: Potential Reflected XSS
high.description: User input appears to be directly output to the HTML response without proper encoding or sanitization. This can lead to reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) if the input contains malicious scripts.
high.code_snippet:
# Dangerous patterns: output of request data without escaping
    REFLECTED_PATTERNS = [
        r'echo\s*\$\_(GET|POST|REQUEST)\[',               # PHP: echo $_GET[...]
        r'print\s*\$\_(GET|POST|REQUEST)\[',
        r'\<\?=\s*\$\_(GET|POST|REQUEST)\[',               # PHP short echo tag
high.remediation: Always escape output based on the context (HTML, JavaScript, URL, etc.). Use a templating engine that auto-escapes, or manually apply functions like htmlspecialchars() in PHP, or equivalent in other languages.

medium.title: Hardcoded Weak Password
medium.remediation: Do not hardcode passwords. Use environment variables or secure configuration management.

medium.title: Weak Encryption
medium.description: Use of weak or deprecated cryptographic algorithm: md5
medium.remediation: Use strong, modern algorithms like AES-256, SHA-256, and avoid ECB mode. Use secure key management.

medium.title: Weak Encryption
medium.description: Use of weak or deprecated cryptographic algorithm: ECB
medium.remediation: Use strong, modern algorithms like AES-256, SHA-256, and avoid ECB mode. Use secure key management.

low.title: Missing Security Headers
low.description: No security headers (X-Frame-Options, CSP, etc.) found in the code. This may lead to clickjacking, MIME type sniffing, etc.
low.remediation: Add security headers like X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, and HSTS.

low.remediation: Add security headers like X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, and HSTS.

findings.title: Potential SQL Injection
findings.description: A SQL query appears to be constructed using string concatenation or interpolation with user-controlled data. This can lead to SQL injection if the input is not properly sanitized.
findings.code_snippet:
with open(self.db_path, "w") as f:
                json.dump({"vulnerabilities": vulnerabilities}, f, indent=2)
            logger.info(f"Updated vulnerability database with {len(vulnerabilities)} entries.")
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to update vulnerability database: {e}")
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead of string concatenation. Escape user inputs if dynamic SQL is unavoidable.

findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead of string concatenation. Escape user inputs if dynamic SQL is unavoidable.

findings.title: Potential Reflected XSS
findings.description: User input appears to be directly output to the HTML response without proper encoding or sanitization. This can lead to reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) if the input contains malicious scripts.
findings.code_snippet:
"""
    Detects reflected XSS patterns:
    - Output of user input without proper encoding (e.g., echo $_GET['x'])
    - Direct rendering of request parameters in HTML
    """
findings.remediation: Always escape output based on the context (HTML, JavaScript, URL, etc.). Use a templating engine that auto-escapes, or manually apply functions like htmlspecialchars() in PHP, or equivalent in other languages.

findings.title: Potential Reflected XSS
findings.description: User input appears to be directly output to the HTML response without proper encoding or sanitization. This can lead to reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) if the input contains malicious scripts.
findings.code_snippet:
# Dangerous patterns: output of request data without escaping
    REFLECTED_PATTERNS = [
        r'echo\s*\$\_(GET|POST|REQUEST)\[',               # PHP: echo $_GET[...]
        r'print\s*\$\_(GET|POST|REQUEST)\[',
        r'\<\?=\s*\$\_(GET|POST|REQUEST)\[',               # PHP short echo tag
findings.remediation: Always escape output based on the context (HTML, JavaScript, URL, etc.). Use a templating engine that auto-escapes, or manually apply functions like htmlspecialchars() in PHP, or equivalent in other languages.

findings.title: Hardcoded Weak Password
findings.description: Hardcoded weak password 'admin' found.
findings.remediation: Do not hardcode passwords. Use environment variables or secure configuration management.

findings.title: Weak Encryption
findings.description: Use of weak or deprecated cryptographic algorithm: md5
findings.remediation: Use strong, modern algorithms like AES-256, SHA-256, and avoid ECB mode. Use secure key management.

findings.title: Weak Encryption
findings.description: Use of weak or deprecated cryptographic algorithm: ECB
findings.remediation: Use strong, modern algorithms like AES-256, SHA-256, and avoid ECB mode. Use secure key management.
